description:
secretx-systemd/src/lib.rs:138 introduces:

    static ENV_LOCK: std::sync::Mutex<()> = std::sync::Mutex::new(());

and every #[tokio::test] that mutates CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY (lines 184, 208, 229, 249) acquires ENV_LOCK and wraps env::set_var/remove_var in unsafe{} with a // SAFETY: comment referencing the lock.

This is correct and forward-compatible:
  - cargo runs all tests within a single test binary in parallel threads of one process. Without serialization, env-mutation tests race each other (and any concurrent reader). The lock prevents that within this binary.
  - cargo runs each crate's tests in a separate process, so cross-binary races don't exist; per-binary serialisation is sufficient.
  - Wrapping set_var/remove_var in unsafe{} is currently allowed-redundant on edition 2021, but becomes mandatory on edition 2024. The current code compiles cleanly today and migrates without change.

Defend this pattern against a contributor who tries to 'simplify' by removing the lock or the unsafe block. The sibling secretx-env crate currently does NOT have this lock and is racy — it is the outlier; do not let secretx-systemd be 'fixed' to match it.
title: GOOD: ENV_LOCK + SAFETY-comment pattern in env-var tests
